1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-027
4	  depends_on:
5	    - ADR-004
6	    - SD-004
7	    - FEAT-004
8	---
9	# Technical Design: Bead-Backed Collection Abstraction
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	This design picks the concrete implementation choices needed to land
14	ADR-004 ("Use Bead-Backed Collections for DDx Runtime Storage") on top
15	of the existing single-file `.ddx/beads.jsonl` tracker described in
16	SD-004 and required by FEAT-004.
17	
18	ADR-004 commits DDx to a model of named bead-backed collections with
19	sidecar attachments. SD-004 describes the existing single-file store and
20	its locking, repair, and append-only evidence semantics. FEAT-004 sets
21	the user-visible behavior of the active tracker. This TD specifies:
22	
23	(a) the collection registry shape,
24	(b) the archival trigger policy and parameters,
25	(c) the attachment storage layout,
26	(d) the migration semantics for the existing 5.4 MB `beads.jsonl`,
27	(e) the read-path semantics across active plus archive, and
28	(f) the bd/br interchange compatibility for the archive collection.
29	
30	The decisions below are the defaults DDx will ship. Anything outside
31	this list — for example new collections beyond `beads`, `beads-archive`,
32	`exec-runs`, and `agent-sessions` — is out of scope for this TD and
33	should be handled in a follow-up TD that depends on this one.
34	
35	## (a) Collection Registry
36	
37	### Registry Shape
38	
39	A collection is a named logical store backed by one of the existing
40	bead backends. The registry is an in-process Go map seeded at startup
41	from a small declarative table. It is not a user-editable file; users
42	do not name new collections, and DDx does not auto-discover collections
43	from disk.
44	
45	```go
46	type CollectionID string
47	
48	type CollectionSpec struct {
49	    ID              CollectionID // "beads", "beads-archive", ...
50	    DefaultBackend  BackendKind  // jsonl | bd | br
51	    JSONLPath       string       // path under .ddx/ for the jsonl backend
52	    QueueSemantics  bool         // true for "beads" only
53	    ArchivePartner  CollectionID // "beads-archive" for "beads", "" otherwise
54	    Attachments     bool         // true for collections that allow sidecars
55	}
56	```
57	
58	Concrete shipping registry:
59	
60	| ID               | JSONL path                       | QueueSemantics | ArchivePartner | Attachments |
61	|------------------|----------------------------------|----------------|----------------|-------------|
62	| `beads`          | `.ddx/beads.jsonl`               | yes            | `beads-archive`| no          |
63	| `beads-archive`  | `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`       | no             | (none)         | no          |
64	| `exec-runs`      | `.ddx/exec-runs.jsonl`           | no             | (none)         | yes         |
65	| `agent-sessions` | `.ddx/agent-sessions.jsonl`      | no             | (none)         | yes         |
66	
67	### Store API
68	
69	The current `beads.Store` API is generalized so the file path is no
70	longer hardcoded. The minimum surface is:
71	
72	```go
73	type Store interface {
74	    Collection(id CollectionID) Collection
75	}
76	
77	type Collection interface {
78	    List(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
79	    Get(ctx context.Context, id string) (Bead, error)
80	    Append(ctx context.Context, b Bead) error
81	    Update(ctx context.Context, b Bead) error
82	    Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error
83	    Lock(ctx context.Context) (Unlocker, error)
84	}
85	```
86	
87	`Lock` is per-collection so that archiving from `beads` to
88	`beads-archive` can hold both locks in a fixed order (`beads` first,
89	then `beads-archive`) to avoid deadlocks.
90	
91	The existing locking, atomic temp-file rename, malformed-record repair,
92	and `Extra` preservation behaviors from SD-004 carry over unchanged on
93	a per-collection basis. Each JSONL collection has its own `.lock`,
94	`.tmp`, and `.bak` files alongside its primary file.
95	
96	### Backend Selection
97	
98	`bd` and `br` backends already accept a collection name through their
99	own CLIs. The DDx external-backend adapter passes the collection ID
100	through to those CLIs. For backends that do not support multiple named
101	collections, the adapter returns a clear error when a non-default
102	collection is requested rather than silently merging records.
103	
104	## (b) Archival Trigger Policy
105	
106	### Goal
107	
108	Keep the active queue small enough that `ddx bead list` and the SD-004
109	read path stay under the 100 ms / 10,000 bead target stated in SD-004,
110	without forcing operators to think about archival.
111	
112	### Trigger
113	
114	Archival runs on demand through `ddx bead archive` and opportunistically
115	after any close-causing mutation when the active collection exceeds a
116	threshold. There is no background daemon.
117	
118	### Defaults
119	
120	| Parameter                      | Default                                |
121	|--------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
122	| `archive.enabled`              | `true`                                 |
123	| `archive.min_age`              | `30d` since `closed_at`                |
124	| `archive.min_active_count`     | `2000` records in `beads`              |
125	| `archive.batch_size`           | `500` per opportunistic pass           |
126	| `archive.statuses`             | `closed`, `wont_fix`, `superseded`     |
127	| `archive.opportunistic`        | `true` (run after close mutations)     |
128	| `archive.preserve_dependencies`| `true` (keep edges resolvable)         |
129	
130	A bead is eligible to archive when its `status` is in
131	`archive.statuses`, its `closed_at` (or `updated_at` fallback) is older
132	than `archive.min_age`, and no open bead in `beads` lists it as a
133	dependency. The dependency check prevents archiving a closed bead that
134	an open bead still references; archived beads remain readable, but
135	queue derivation in SD-004 should not have to load `beads-archive` to
136	decide `ready`/`blocked`.
137	
138	### Configuration
139	
140	Defaults live in `config.go` and may be overridden through `.ddx.yml`:
141	
142	```yaml
143	archive:
144	  enabled: true
145	  min_age: 30d
146	  min_active_count: 2000
147	  batch_size: 500
148	  statuses: [closed, wont_fix, superseded]
149	  opportunistic: true
150	```
151	
152	### Mutation Sequence
153	
154	1. Acquire `beads` lock.
155	2. Acquire `beads-archive` lock.
156	3. Read both snapshots.
157	4. Select up to `batch_size` eligible records from `beads`.
158	5. Append the selected records to `beads-archive` with an added
159	   `archived_at` timestamp in `Extra`.
160	6. Remove the selected records from `beads`.
161	7. Atomic temp-file rename for `beads-archive` first, then for `beads`.
162	   This ordering means a crash mid-archive leaves a record in both
163	   collections rather than nowhere, and the read path in (e) hides the
164	   duplicate behind active-wins precedence.
165	8. Release locks in reverse order.
166	
167	## (c) Attachment Storage Layout
168	
169	### Layout
170	
171	Attachments are sidecar files under a per-collection directory keyed by
172	record ID:
173	
174	```
175	.ddx/attachments/
176	  <collection-id>/
177	    <record-id>/
178	      events.jsonl
179	      prompt.txt
180	      response.txt
181	      stdout.log
182	      <name>.<ext>
183	```
184	
185	Concrete defaults:
186	
187	- The active `beads` collection does not use sidecar attachments.
188	  Evidence stays in `Extra["events"]` per SD-004, because that history
189	  is small and is referenced often by queue tooling.
190	- `exec-runs` and `agent-sessions` use sidecars by default for prompt,
191	  response, stdout, stderr, and structured result blobs.
192	- `beads-archive` does not introduce new attachments. Records moved
193	  from `beads` carry their existing `Extra["events"]` history inline.
194	
195	### Reference Format
196	
197	Attachment references are stored under a reserved
198	`Extra["attachments"]` array on the record:
199	
200	```json
201	{
202	  "attachments": [
203	    {
204	      "name": "prompt",
205	      "path": "exec-runs/run-2026-05-01-abc/prompt.txt",
206	      "media_type": "text/plain",
207	      "sha256": "…",
208	      "size": 12345
209	    }
210	  ]
211	}
212	```
213	
214	`path` is repository-relative under `.ddx/attachments/`. `sha256` and
215	`size` are recorded at write time and verified on read. The reference
216	key `attachments` is DDx-specific and lives in preserved extras as
217	required by ADR-004; it does not rename or shadow any bd/br field.
218	
219	### Write Algorithm
220	
221	1. Write the attachment to a temp path under
222	   `.ddx/attachments/<collection>/<id>/.<name>.tmp`.
223	2. `fsync`, then rename to the final path.
224	3. Compute and record `sha256` and `size`.
225	4. Append the reference to `Extra["attachments"]`.
226	5. Persist the record through the normal collection write path so the
227	   reference becomes durable atomically with the temp-file rename of
228	   the collection JSONL file.
229	
230	### Garbage Collection
231	
232	Attachments are removed only when their owning record is removed. A
233	`ddx bead gc` command (out of scope for this TD beyond the contract)
234	walks `.ddx/attachments/<collection>/` and deletes directories whose
235	record IDs no longer exist in the collection. There is no time-based
236	attachment expiration.
237	
238	## (d) Migration Plan for Existing `beads.jsonl`
239	
240	The current `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is 5.4 MB and 1,172 lines. The
241	migration must be safe to run on an in-place repository checkout.
242	
243	### Steps
244	
245	1. **Compatibility shim first.** The new collection registry treats
246	   `.ddx/beads.jsonl` as the JSONL path for the `beads` collection. No
247	   file move is required to keep the active tracker working. Existing
248	   readers and writers continue to operate against the same path.
249	
250	2. **Idempotent create-on-write for archive.** The
251	   `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` file is created lazily on the first
252	   archive operation. Absence of the file is treated as an empty
253	   archive collection.
254	
255	3. **One-shot backfill command.** `ddx bead migrate-archive` performs
256	   the initial archival pass:
257	   - Acquire both locks.
258	   - Make a backup copy of `.ddx/beads.jsonl` to
259	     `.ddx/beads.jsonl.pre-archive.bak`.
260	   - Move all records that match the archival policy in (b) using the
261	     normal mutation sequence.
262	   - Print a summary of moved/retained counts.
263	   This command is opt-in. Users who never run it keep the current
264	   single-file behavior.
265	
266	4. **No schema rewrite.** Existing records are not rewritten to add
267	   new fields. The `attachments` extra appears only on new
268	   `exec-runs` and `agent-sessions` records.
269	
270	5. **Rollback.** `ddx bead migrate-archive --rollback` re-merges
271	   `beads-archive.jsonl` into `beads.jsonl` under both locks, atomic
272	   temp-file rename, and removes the archive file when empty.
273	
274	6. **Doc audit.** The migration command is documented in the bead
275	   command reference. `ddx doc audit` should pass after this TD lands
276	   because the new TD declares its dependencies and no existing artifact
277	   needs to change its `depends_on`.
278	
279	### Risk Mitigation
280	
281	- The default `archive.min_active_count` of 2000 means existing
282	  installations under that count will not be archived opportunistically
283	  on close. They keep behaving like today's single-file tracker until
284	  they cross the threshold or run `ddx bead migrate-archive`.
285	- The pre-archive backup file is never removed automatically.
286	
287	## (e) Read-Path Semantics Across Active and Archive
288	
289	### Default View
290	
291	Queue commands defined by SD-004 (`list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `status`)
292	read only the `beads` collection. They do not load `beads-archive`.
293	This preserves the SD-004 100 ms target as the active set stays bounded
294	and matches user expectation that "what's on the queue" is the active
295	queue.
296	
297	### Merged View
298	
299	`ddx bead show <id>`, `ddx bead history`, and any explicit
300	`--include-archive` flag use a merged view:
301	
302	1. Look up the bead in `beads` first.
303	2. Fall back to `beads-archive` if not found.
304	3. For listing operations with `--include-archive`, lazily concatenate
305	   the two snapshots and de-duplicate by `id` with active-wins
306	   precedence (so a record present in both collections after an
307	   interrupted archive is reported as the active copy).
308	
309	### Lazy Loading
310	
311	The archive collection is opened only when a merged view is requested.
312	The store keeps per-collection snapshots cached for the lifetime of a
313	single command invocation; commands do not share snapshots across
314	invocations.
315	
316	### Deletion Semantics
317	
318	`ddx bead delete <id>` removes from the collection that currently holds
319	the record. If the record is in both collections (post-crash), the
320	delete removes it from both under both locks before returning success.
321	
322	### Dependency Resolution
323	
324	When the queue derivation in SD-004 needs to display a closed
325	dependency that has been archived, it reads only the dependency's
326	`status` from `beads-archive` on demand and caches it in memory for the
327	remainder of the command. It never promotes archived beads back into
328	the active snapshot.
329	
330	## (f) bd/br Interchange Compatibility for the Archive Collection
331	
332	### Constraints from ADR-004
333	
334	ADR-004 requires that bd/br interchange continue to work through
335	`list --json` for reads and `import --from jsonl --replace -` for
336	writes, and that the bead envelope keeps its shared field names.
337	
338	### Decision
339	
340	The archive collection participates in interchange on the same terms
341	as the active collection.
342	
343	- `ddx bead export --collection beads-archive` emits one bead-record
344	  JSON object per line, identical schema to active beads.
345	- `ddx bead import --collection beads-archive --from jsonl -` accepts
346	  the same shape.
347	- `archived_at` is stored in `Extra` and round-trips as an unknown
348	  field for bd/br, exactly like other DDx-specific fields. It is not
349	  promoted to a top-level field.
350	- The `attachments` extra also round-trips. bd/br do not interpret it,
351	  but they do not strip preserved extras, so references survive an
352	  export/import cycle.
353	
354	### Adapter Behavior
355	
356	For backends that support multiple named collections, the DDx adapter
357	passes the collection ID through. For backends that do not, the
358	adapter returns an error when the user asks for a non-default
359	collection rather than silently merging records into the default
360	collection. This is the same rule as (a).
361	
362	### Schema Compatibility Test
363	
364	`schema_compat_test.go` is extended with an "archive round-trip" case:
365	take a record from the active collection, archive it, export it,
366	re-import it into a fresh `beads-archive` collection, and verify
367	field-for-field equality including preserved extras.
368	
369	## Validation
370	
371	These checks must pass after this TD is implemented:
372	
373	- `go test ./internal/bead/...`
374	- `go test ./cmd -run 'TestBeadCollection|TestArchive|TestAttachment' -count=1`
375	- `ddx doc audit` shows TD-027 with no broken edges.
376	- Manual: run `ddx bead migrate-archive` on a copy of an existing
377	  repository checkout and confirm queue commands produce the same
378	  active snapshot they did before migration.
379	
380	## Non-Goals
381	
382	- Adding collections beyond `beads`, `beads-archive`, `exec-runs`, and
383	  `agent-sessions`. Future collections require a follow-up TD.
384	- Real-time replication between collections.
385	- Cross-repository archive sharing.
386	- Background or scheduled archival; archival only runs on user-initiated
387	  commands or as an opportunistic step inside an already-mutating
388	  command.
389

1	package bead
2	
3	// Backend defines the storage interface for beads.
4	// The JSONL backend is the default. The bd and br backends shell out
5	// to external tools with the same bead-compatible interface.
6	type Backend interface {
7		Init() error
8		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
9		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
10		WithLock(fn func() error) error
11	}
12	
13	// BackendType constants
14	const (
15		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
16		BackendBD    = "bd"
17		BackendBR    = "br"
18	)
19	
20	const DefaultCollection = "beads"
21

68	func TestExternalBackendCarriesLogicalCollectionName(t *testing.T) {

112		b := &Bead{Title: "Fix auth bug", IssueType: "bug", Priority: 1}
113		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
114	
115		assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
116		assert.True(t, len(b.ID) > 3, "ID should have prefix + hex")
117		assert.Equal(t, "bug", b.IssueType)
118		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, b.Status)
119		assert.Equal(t, 1, b.Priority)
120		assert.False(t, b.CreatedAt.IsZero())
121	
122		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
123		require.NoError(t, err)
124		assert.Equal(t, b.Title, got.Title)
125		assert.Equal(t, b.IssueType, got.IssueType)
126	}

139	bead:

141	`), 0o644))

149		assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(b.ID, "nif-"))

data.text: Now I have a clear picture. The Store already supports named collections by convention (`<name>.jsonl`); what TD-027 wants is an explicit registry. I'll add a small `registry.go` defining `CollectionSpec` and `DefaultRegistry`, and have `NewStore` consult it (with fallback for unregistered names so existing semantics survive).
Conflict: `DefaultCollection` is a const, but I want a `DefaultRegistry` function. No conflict actually—different names. But I'm shadowing `defaultRegistry`'s init expression. Let me check.

3. ✅ Bead store tests pass — `go test ./internal/bead/...` green.
4. ✅ `schema_compat_test.go` green (part of bead suite).
5. ✅ No new collections instantiated — registry ships only `beads`; `TestDefaultRegistryShipsBeadsOnly` enforces it.

input.command:
git add internal/bead/registry.go internal/bead/registry_test.go internal/bead/store.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(bead): introduce collection registry [ddx-9cc3abad]

Add an in-process registry that maps logical collection IDs to concrete
JSONL file paths and lock directories, per TD-027 §(a). NewStore now
resolves its file/lock paths through DefaultRegistry instead of
hardcoding "<collection>.jsonl"/.lock". The shipping registry contains
only the active "beads" collection; archive and sidecar collections are
deferred to C3.

Unregistered collection names continue to work via Resolve's
"<id>.jsonl" fallback so existing call sites (exec-runs, agent-sessions
in tests) keep their semantics unchanged.
EOF
)"
